582		end, { description = "move to screen", group = "client" }),
583		awful.key({ MODKEY, "Shift" }, "y", function(c)
584			c.ontop = not c.ontop
585		end, { description = "toggle keep on top", group = "client" }),
586		awful.key({ MODKEY }, "y", function(c)
587			c.sticky = not c.sticky
588		end, { description = "toggle sticky", group = "client" }),
589		awful.key({ MODKEY }, "n", function(c)

593		end, { description = "minimize", group = "client" }),
594		awful.key({ MODKEY }, "m", function(c)
595			c.maximized = not c.maximized
596			c:raise()
597		end, { description = "(un)maximize", group = "client" }),

608	-- This should map on the top row of your keyboard, usually 1 to 9.
609	for i = 1, 9 do

630				if client.focus then

651			c:emit_signal("request::activate", "mouse_click", { raise = true })
652		end),

663	-- Set keys
664	root.keys(GLOBALKEYS)

667	-- {{{ Rules
668	-- Rules to apply to new clients (through the "manage" signal).
669	
670	-- Function to check the number of displays

673	-- end

675	-- -- Define the rules based on the number of displays

679	-- 	table.insert(rules, { rule = { class = "Brave-browser" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "2" } })
680	-- 	table.insert(rules, { rule = { class = "kitty" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "1" } })
681	-- 	table.insert(rules, { rule = { class = "Postman" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "3" } })
682	-- 	table.insert(rules, { rule = { class = "Slack" }, properties = { screen = 2, tag = "1" } })
683	-- else
684	-- table.insert(rules, { rule = { class = "Brave-browser" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "2" } })
685	-- table.insert(rules, { rule = { class = "kitty" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "1" } })
686	-- table.insert(rules, { rule = { class = "Postman" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "3" } })
687	-- table.insert(rules, { rule = { class = "Slack" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "4" } })
688	-- end
689	
690	-- Check if this is a theme switch restart or normal restart
691	local is_theme_switch = io.open("/tmp/awesome_theme_switch", "r") ~= nil
692	local is_normal_restart = io.open("/tmp/awesome_normal_restart", "r") ~= nil
693	local is_any_restart = is_theme_switch or is_normal_restart
694	
695	awful.rules.rules = {
696		-- All clients will match this rule.
697		{
698			rule = {},
699			properties = {
700				border_width = beautiful.border_width,
701				border_color = beautiful.border_normal,
702				focus = awful.client.focus.filter,
703				raise = true,
704				keys = CLIENTKEYS,
705				buttons = clientbuttons,
706				screen = awful.screen.preferred,
707				placement = awful.placement.no_overlap + awful.placement.no_offscreen,
708			},
709		},
710	
711		-- Only apply tag rules if this is NOT any kind of restart
712		{ rule = { class = "kitty" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "1" }, rule_any = { is_any_restart = false } },
713		{ rule = { class = "obsidian" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "2" }, rule_any = { is_any_restart = false } },
714		{
715			rule = { class = "Brave-browser" },
716			properties = { screen = 1, tag = "3" },
717			rule_any = { is_any_restart = false },
718		},
719		{ rule = { class = "bruno" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "4" }, rule_any = { is_any_restart = false } },
720		{ rule = { class = "firefox" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "5" }, rule_any = { is_any_restart = false } },
721		{ rule = { class = "Slack" }, properties = { screen = 1, tag = "6" }, rule_any = { is_any_restart = false } },
722	
723		-- Add titlebars to normal clients and dialogs
724		{ rule_any = { type = { "normal", "dialog" } }, properties = { titlebars_enabled = false } },
725	}
726	-- }}}
727	
728	-- {{{ Signals
729	-- Signal function to execute when a new client appears.
730	client.connect_signal("manage", function(c)
731		-- Set the windows at the slave,
732		-- i.e. put it at the end of others instead of setting it master.
733		-- if not awesome.startup then awful.client.setslave(c) end
734	
735		if awesome.startup and not c.size_hints.user_position and not c.size_hints.program_position then
736			-- Prevent clients from being unreachable after screen count changes.
737			awful.placement.no_offscreen(c)
738		end
739	
740		-- Don't focus this client if we're restoring a tag
741		if _restoring_tag then
742			c:emit_signal("request::activate", "manage", { raise = false })
743		end
744	end)
745	
746	-- Add a titlebar if titlebars_enabled is set to true in the rules.
747	client.connect_signal("request::titlebars", function(c)
748		-- Custom
749		if beautiful.titlebar_fun then
750			beautiful.titlebar_fun(c)
751			return
752		end
753	
754		-- buttons for the titlebar
755		local buttons = gears.table.join(
756			awful.button({}, 1, function()
757				c:emit_signal("request::activate", "titlebar", { raise = true })
758				awful.mouse.client.move(c)
759			end),
760			awful.button({}, 3, function()
761				c:emit_signal("request::activate", "titlebar", { raise = true })
762				awful.mouse.client.resize(c)
763			end)
764		)
765	
766		awful.titlebar(c):setup({
767			{ -- Left
768				awful.titlebar.widget.iconwidget(c),
769				buttons = buttons,
770				layout = wibox.layout.fixed.horizontal,
771			},
772			{ -- Middle
773				-- { -- Title
774				--     align  = "center",
775				--     widget = awful.titlebar.widget.titlewidget(c)
776				-- },
777				-- buttons = buttons,
778				-- layout = wibox.layout.flex.horizontal,
779			},
780			{ -- Right
781				awful.titlebar.widget.floatingbutton(c),
782				awful.titlebar.widget.maximizedbutton(c),
783				awful.titlebar.widget.stickybutton(c),
784				awful.titlebar.widget.ontopbutton(c),
785				awful.titlebar.widget.closebutton(c),
786				layout = wibox.layout.fixed.horizontal(),
787			},
788			layout = wibox.layout.align.horizontal,
789		})
790	end)
791	
792	-- Enable sloppy focus, so that focus follows mouse.
793	client.connect_signal("mouse::enter", function(c)
794		c:emit_signal("request::activate", "mouse_enter", { raise = false })
795	end)
796	
797	client.connect_signal("focus", function(c)
798		c.border_color = beautiful.border_focus
799	end)
800	client.connect_signal("unfocus", function(c)
801		c.border_color = beautiful.border_normal
802	end)
803	-- }}}

1	-------------------------------------------------
2	-- Net Speed Widget for Awesome Window Manager
3	-- Shows current upload/download speed
4	-- More details could be found here:
5	-- https://github.com/streetturtle/awesome-wm-widgets/tree/master/net-speed-widget
6	
7	-- @author Pavel Makhov
8	-- @copyright 2020 Pavel Makhov
9	-------------------------------------------------
10	
11	local watch = require("awful.widget.watch")
12	local wibox = require("wibox")
13	local beautiful = require("beautiful")
14	local markup = require("lain.util.markup")
15	
16	local HOME_DIR = os.getenv("HOME")
17	local WIDGET_DIR = HOME_DIR .. "/.config/awesome/widgets/net_speed_widget/"
18	local ICONS_DIR = WIDGET_DIR .. "icons/"
19	
20	local net_speed_widget = {}
21	
22	local function convert_to_h(bytes)
23		local speed
24		local dim
25		local bits = bytes * 8
26		if bits < 1000 then
27			speed = bits
28			dim = "b/s"
29		elseif bits < 1000000 then
30			speed = bits / 1000
31			dim = "kb/s"
32		elseif bits < 1000000000 then
33			speed = bits / 1000000
34			dim = "Mb/s"
35		elseif bits < 1000000000000 then
36			speed = bits / 1000000000
37			dim = "Gb/s"
38		else
39			speed = tonumber(bits)
40			dim = "b/s"
41		end
42		return math.floor(speed + 0.5) .. " " .. dim
43	end
44	
45	local function split(string_to_split, separator)
46		if separator == nil then
47			separator = "%s"
48		end
49		local t = {}
50	
51		for str in string.gmatch(string_to_split, "([^" .. separator .. "]+)") do
52			table.insert(t, str)
53		end
54	
55		return t
56	end
57	
58	local function worker(user_args)
59		local args = user_args or {}
60	
61		local interface = args.interface or "*"
62		local timeout = args.timeout or 2
63		local width = args.width or 55
64	
65		net_speed_widget = wibox.widget({
66			{
67				id = "rx_speed",
68				forced_width = width,
69				align = "right",
70				widget = wibox.widget.textbox,
71				bg = "#FFFF00",
72			},
73			{
74				image = ICONS_DIR .. "down.svg",
75				widget = wibox.widget.imagebox,
76				bg = "#FFFF00",
77			},
78			{
79				image = ICONS_DIR .. "up.svg",
80				widget = wibox.widget.imagebox,
81				bg = "#FFFF00",
82			},
83			{
84				id = "tx_speed",
85				forced_width = width,
86				align = "left",
87				widget = wibox.widget.textbox,
88				bg = "#FFFF00",
89			},
90			layout = wibox.layout.fixed.horizontal,
91			set_rx_text = function(self, new_rx_speed)
92				self:get_children_by_id("rx_speed")[1]:set_markup(markup.font(beautiful.font, markup.fg.color(beautiful.fg_normal, tostring(new_rx_speed))))
93			end,
94			set_tx_text = function(self, new_tx_speed)
95				self:get_children_by_id("tx_speed")[1]:set_markup(markup.font(beautiful.font, markup.fg.color(beautiful.fg_normal, tostring(new_tx_speed))))
96			end,
97		})
98	
99		-- make sure these are not shared across different worker/widgets (e.g. two monitors)
100		-- otherwise the speed will be randomly split among the worker in each monitor
101		local prev_rx = 0
102		local prev_tx = 0
103	
104		local update_widget = function(widget, stdout)
105			local cur_vals = split(stdout, "\r\n")
106	
107			local cur_rx = 0
108			local cur_tx = 0
109	
110			for i, v in ipairs(cur_vals) do
111				if i % 2 == 1 then
112					cur_rx = cur_rx + v
113				end
114				if i % 2 == 0 then
115					cur_tx = cur_tx + v
116				end
117			end
118	
119			local speed_rx = (cur_rx - prev_rx) / timeout
120			local speed_tx = (cur_tx - prev_tx) / timeout
121	
122			widget:set_rx_text(convert_to_h(speed_rx))
123			widget:set_tx_text(convert_to_h(speed_tx))
124	
125			prev_rx = cur_rx
126			prev_tx = cur_tx
127		end
128	
129		watch(
130			string.format([[bash -c "cat /sys/class/net/%s/statistics/*_bytes"]], interface),
131			timeout,
132			update_widget,
133			net_speed_widget
134		)
135	
136		return net_speed_widget
137	end
138	
139	return setmetatable(net_speed_widget, {
140		__call = function(_, ...)
141			return worker(...)
142		end,
143	})
144

content.text: Let me also check one of the more complex widgets to see patterns for advanced implementations:

content.content:
1	-- Music widget using playerctl (for browser/Spotify/etc)
2	local wibox = require("wibox")
3	local awful = require("awful")
4	local markup = require("lain.util.markup")
5	local gears = require("gears")
6	
7	local music_text = wibox.widget.textbox()
8	music_text.align = "center"
9	music_text.forced_width = 24
10	local album_art = wibox.widget.imagebox()
11	
12	local text_scroll = wibox.container.scroll.horizontal(music_text, 40)
13	local text_area = wibox.container.constraint(text_scroll, "exact", 24, nil)
14	
15	-- Create control buttons
16	local prev_button = wibox.widget.textbox()
17	local next_button = wibox.widget.textbox()
18	
19	-- Set initial button text with everforest colors
20	prev_button:set_markup(markup.font(theme.font, markup.fg.color("#A7C080", "⏮\u{FE0E}")))
21	next_button:set_markup(markup.font(theme.font, markup.fg.color("#A7C080", "⏭\u{FE0E}")))
22	
23	-- Set initial state: collapsed with music note
24	prev_button.visible = false
25	next_button.visible = false
26	music_text:set_markup(markup.font(theme.font, markup.fg.color("#D3C6AA", "♪")))
27	
28	local function update_music()
29		-- Query all players, get the first one that is Playing or Paused
30		awful.spawn.easy_async_with_shell(
31			[[playerctl -a metadata --format '{{status}}|||{{artist}}|||{{title}}|||{{xesam:url}}|||{{mpris:artUrl}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -m1 '^Playing\|^Paused']],
32			function(stdout)
33				stdout = stdout:gsub("\n", ""):gsub("\r", ""):gsub("^%s*(.-)%s*$", "%1")
34	
35				if stdout == "" then
36					album_art:set_image(nil)
37					prev_button.visible = false
38					next_button.visible = false
39					text_area.width = 24
40					music_text.forced_width = 24
41					music_text.align = "center"
42					music_text:set_markup(markup.font(theme.font, markup.fg.color("#859289", "♪")))
43					return
44				end
45	
46				local artist, title, url, art_url = stdout:match("^[^|]+|||(.-)|||(.-)|||(.-)|||(.-)$")
47				artist = artist or ""
48				title = title or ""
49				url = url or ""
50				art_url = art_url or ""
51	
52				-- Fallback to filename from URL if title is empty
53				if title == "" and url ~= "" then
54					title = url:match("([^/]+)$") or url
55					title = title:gsub("%%(%x%x)", function(h) return string.char(tonumber(h, 16)) end)
56					title = title:gsub("%.[^%.]+$", "")
57				end
58	
59				local song
60				if artist ~= "" and title ~= "" then
61					song = artist .. " - " .. title
62				elseif title ~= "" then
63					song = title
64				else
65					song = ""
66				end
67	
68				if song ~= "" and string.len(song) > 3 then
69					prev_button.visible = true
70					next_button.visible = true
71					text_area.width = 150
72					music_text.forced_width = nil
73					music_text.align = "left"
74					if art_url ~= "" and art_url ~= "file://" then
75						album_art:set_image(art_url:gsub("file://", ""))
76					else
77						album_art:set_image(nil)
78					end
79					local display = song:gsub("&", "&"):gsub("<", "<"):gsub(">", ">")
80					music_text:set_markup(markup.font(theme.font, markup.fg.color("#D3C6AA", " " .. display .. " ")))
81				else
82					album_art:set_image(nil)
83					prev_button.visible = false
84					next_button.visible = false
85					text_area.width = 24
86					music_text.forced_width = 24
87					music_text.align = "center"
88					music_text:set_markup(markup.font(theme.font, markup.fg.color("#859289", "♪")))
89				end
90			end
91		)
92	end
93	
94	local music_widget = wibox.container.background(
95		wibox.container.margin(
96			wibox.widget({
97				-- Left side with prev button
98				{
99					prev_button,
100					layout = wibox.layout.fixed.horizontal,
101				},
102				-- Center with album art and scrolling music text
103				{
104					album_art,
105					text_area,
106					layout = wibox.layout.fixed.horizontal,
107				},
108				-- Right side with next button
109				{
110					next_button,
111					layout = wibox.layout.fixed.horizontal,
112				},
113				layout = wibox.layout.align.horizontal,
114			}),
115			2,
116			4
117		),
118		"#425047",
119		gears.shape.rounded_rect
120	)
121	
122	-- Make buttons clickable
123	prev_button:buttons(awful.util.table.join(awful.button({}, 1, function()
124		awful.spawn("playerctl previous")
125		-- Add a small delay before updating to allow the player to change
126		gears.timer.delayed_call(function()
127			update_music()
128		end)
129	end)))
130	
131	next_button:buttons(awful.util.table.join(awful.button({}, 1, function()
132		awful.spawn("playerctl next")
133		-- Add a small delay before updating to allow the player to change
134		gears.timer.delayed_call(function()
135			update_music()
136		end)
137	end)))
138	
139	-- Make music text clickable for play/pause
140	local function toggle_play_pause()
141		awful.spawn("playerctl play-pause")
142		gears.timer.delayed_call(function()
143			update_music()
144		end)
145	end
146	music_text:buttons(awful.util.table.join(awful.button({}, 1, toggle_play_pause)))
147	text_scroll:buttons(awful.util.table.join(awful.button({}, 1, toggle_play_pause)))
148	
149	-- Initial update
150	update_music()
151	
152	-- Update every 4 seconds
153	local timer = gears.timer({ timeout = 4 })
154	timer:connect_signal("timeout", update_music)
155	timer:start()
156	
157	return music_widget
158

content.text: Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary. ## Comprehensive AwesomeWM Configuration Summary ### 1. Overall Directory Structure ``` /home/tymoyato/.dotfiles/dotfiles/awesome/ ├── rc.lua # Main config file (28KB) ├── widgets_config/ # Global/fallback widget configs │ ├── battery_widget.lua │ ├── brightness_widget.lua │ ├── clock_widget.lua │ ├── cpu_widget.lua │ ├── mem_widget.lua │ ├── music_widget.lua │ ├── temp_widget.lua │ └── volume_widget.lua ├── widgets/ # Custom widget modules (self-contained) │ ├── kbd_widget/ │ │ └── kbd_widget.lua │ └── net_speed_widget/ │ ├── net_speed.lua │ └── icons/ ├── themes/ # Multiple theme directories │ ├── everforest/ │ │ ├── theme.lua # Main theme file │ │ ├── widgets_config/ # Theme-specific widget configs │ │ ├── icons/ │ │ └── wallpapers/ │ ├── kitay/ │ └── cccp/ ├── lain/ # Lain library (widgets, utilities, layouts) │ ├── widget/ # Pre-built widgets (bat, cpu, mem, etc) │ ├── util/ │ │ ├── markup.lua # Text markup utilities │ │ ├── separators.lua │ │ └── ... │ └── layout/ ├── freedesktop/ # Freedesktop menu integration ├── utils/ # Shell utilities └── scratch/ # Scratch/helper scripts ``` ### 2. Widget Structure & Patterns #### **Simple Widget Pattern** (clock, cpu, brightness) ```lua -- Minimal widget that returns a single container local wibox = require("wibox") local gears = require("gears") local markup = require("lain.util.markup") local awful = require("awful") -- If interactive -- Create core widget (textbox, imagebox, or lain widget) local my_text = wibox.widget.textbox() my_text:set_markup(markup.font(theme.font, markup.fg.color("#color", " text "))) -- Optional: Add interactivity with buttons my_text:buttons(awful.util.table.join( awful.button({}, 1, function() -- left click -- action end) )) -- Wrap in containers (background, margin) local widget = wibox.container.background( wibox.container.margin(my_text, 2, 4), theme.bg_focus, -- background color gears.shape.rounded_rect -- shape ) return widget ``` #### **Lain Widget Pattern** (battery, volume, cpu with icons) ```lua -- Uses lain library widgets which handle updates automatically local wibox = require("wibox") local lain = require("lain") local markup = lain.util.markup -- Optional icon theme.widget_icon = theme.dir .. "/icons/widgets/icon.png" local icon = wibox.widget.imagebox(theme.widget_icon) -- Create lain widget (handles updates via timeout + settings) local core_widget = lain.widget.bat({ -- or cpu, mem, etc timeout = 5, notify = "on", settings = function() -- Update widget display based on *_now variable -- (bat_now, cpu_now, mem_now, etc) widget:set_markup( markup.font( theme.font, markup.fg.color(theme.fg_widget, " " .. bat_now.perc .. "% ") ) ) end, }) -- Combine icon + widget in horizontal layout local widget = wibox.container.background( wibox.container.margin( wibox.widget({ icon, core_widget.widget, layout = wibox.layout.align.horizontal }), 2, 2 ), "#color", gears.shape.rounded_rect ) return widget ``` #### **Module Widget Pattern** (net_speed_widget - reusable) ```lua -- Returns a callable module that can be instantiated with parameters local watch = require("awful.widget.watch") local wibox = require("wibox") local net_speed_widget = {} local function worker(user_args) local args = user_args or {} local timeout = args.timeout or 2 local interface = args.interface or "*" -- Create widget structure net_speed_widget = wibox.widget({ -- nested structure with IDs for targeting id = "rx_speed", -- custom setter methods set_rx_text = function(self, text) ... end, }) -- Watch a command and update widget on timeout watch( string.format([[bash -c "cat /sys/class/net/%s/statistics/*_bytes"]], interface), timeout, update_widget, net_speed_widget ) return net_speed_widget end -- Return as callable metatable return setmetatable(net_speed_widget, { __call = function(_, ...) return worker(...) end, }) ``` ### 3. Widget Registration & Loading **In rc.lua (lines 100-138):** - Loads lain library: `local lain = require("lain")` - No widgets are directly loaded in rc.lua **In theme.lua (everforest example):** ```lua -- Lines 122-137: Require all widgets local clock_widget = require("themes.everforest.widgets_config.clock_widget") local battery_widget = require("themes.everforest.widgets_config.battery_widget") local cpu_widget = require("themes.everforest.widgets_config.cpu_widget") -- Global widgets (same path everywhere) local kbd_widget = require("widgets.kbd_widget.kbd_widget") local net_speed_widget = require("widgets.net_speed_widget.net_speed") -- Lines 139-230: theme.connect() function -- This is called for EACH SCREEN (lines 317-319 of rc.lua) function theme.connect(s) -- Setup wibar (top bar) s.mywibox = awful.wibar({ position = "top", screen = s, height = 16 }) -- Lines 177-229: Add widgets to wibar layout (right side) s.mywibox:setup({ layout = wibox.layout.align.horizontal, { -- Left side s.mytaglist, s.mypromptbox, }, { -- Middle (empty) }, { -- Right side - ALL WIDGETS GO HERE music_widget, wibox.widget.textbox(" "), -- Separator brightness_widget, wibox.widget.textbox(" "), volume_widget, -- ... more widgets ... clock_widget, }, }) end ``` **Key Loading Pattern:** 1. Each theme has its own `theme.lua` file 2. Theme loads theme-specific widgets from `themes/{theme_name}/widgets_config/` 3. Global widgets loaded from `widgets/` directory 4. `theme.connect(s)` is called per-screen and assembles the wibar 5. New widgets must be required AND added to the wibar setup in `theme.connect()` ### 4. Current Theme & Color Variables **Active Theme:** `everforest` (Everforest Dark Medium Contrast) **Core Color Palette (from /themes/everforest/theme.lua):** ```lua -- Core colors theme.bg_normal = "#2D353B" -- Dark background theme.fg_normal = "#D3C6AA" -- Light foreground theme.bg_focus = "#425047" -- Focused background (slightly lighter) theme.fg_focus = "#A7C080" -- Focused foreground (green accent) theme.bg_urgent = "#514045" -- Urgent background (red-tinted) theme.fg_urgent = "#E67E80" -- Urgent text (red) -- Widget-specific theme.fg_widget = "#A7C080" -- Green for widget text -- Semantic colors (Everforest palette) theme.green = "#A7C080" theme.red = "#E67E80" theme.yellow = "#DBBC7F" theme.blue = "#7FBBB3" theme.orange = "#E69875" theme.purple = "#D699B6" theme.aqua = "#83C092" theme.grey0 = "#7A8478" theme.grey1 = "#859289" -- Taglist & Tasklist theme.taglist_bg_focus = "#425047" theme.taglist_fg_focus = "#D3C6AA" theme.taglist_bg_occupied = "#3D484D" theme.taglist_fg_occupied = "#A7C080" -- Font theme.font = "Meslo LGS Regular 10" theme.taglist_font = "Meslo LGS Bold 10" ``` **Common Widget Colors Used:** - Container backgrounds: `"#425047"`, `"#6c5ce7"`, `theme.lightgrey`, `theme.bg_focus` - Text color: `theme.fg_widget` (green #A7C080), `"#D3C6AA"` (light), `"#859289"` (grey) - Shapes: `gears.shape.rounded_rect` (standard) ### 5. Utility Functions & Helpers Available **From lain.util.markup (text styling):** ```lua markup.font(font_name, text) -- Set font markup.fg.color(color, text) -- Foreground color markup.bg.color(color, text) -- Background color markup.color(fg, bg, text) -- Both colors markup.fontfg(font, fg, text) -- Font + foreground markup.bold(text) -- text markup.italic(text) -- text markup.underline(text) -- text ``` **From awful (Awesome library):** ```lua awful.spawn(cmd) -- Run command awful.spawn.with_shell(cmd) -- Run with shell awful.spawn.easy_async_with_shell(cmd, callback) -- Async with callback awful.widget.watch(cmd, timeout, callback, widget) -- Poll command regularly awful.button({modifiers}, button_num, callback) -- Mouse button binding awful.util.table.join(...) -- Merge tables ``` **From gears:** ```lua gears.shape.rounded_rect -- Rounded rectangle shape gears.timer({ timeout = N }) -- Create timer timer:connect_signal("timeout", func) -- Register timeout callback gears.timer.delayed_call(func) -- Execute in next iteration ``` **Wibox Layout Options:** ```lua wibox.layout.fixed.horizontal -- Fixed-size horizontal layout wibox.layout.align.horizontal -- Aligned (left/center/right) wibox.layout.flex.horizontal -- Flexible/stretching layout -- Common widget types wibox.widget.textbox() -- Text widget wibox.widget.imagebox(path) -- Image widget wibox.container.margin(widget, l, r, t, b) -- Add margins wibox.container.background(widget, color, shape) -- Colored background wibox.container.scroll.horizontal(widget, width) -- Scrolling text ``` ### 6. How to Create a New Widget **Step 1: Create widget file** - **Option A (Theme-specific):** `/themes/everforest/widgets_config/my_widget.lua` - **Option B (Global):** `/widgets/my_widget/my_widget.lua` **Step 2: Basic template structure** ```lua -- my_widget.lua local wibox = require("wibox") local awful = require("awful") local gears = require("gears") local markup = require("lain.util.markup") -- Create core widget local my_widget_text = wibox.widget.textbox() -- Update function (if polling) local function update_widget() -- Get data (via awful.spawn, file read, etc) awful.spawn.easy_async_with_shell("your_command", function(stdout) local value = stdout:gsub("\n", "") my_widget_text:set_markup( markup.font( theme.font, markup.fg.color("#D3C6AA", " " .. value .. " ") ) ) end) end -- Initial update update_widget() -- Setup timer for periodic updates local timer = gears.timer({ timeout = 5 }) timer:connect_signal("timeout", update_widget) timer:start() -- Add interactivity (optional) my_widget_text:buttons(awful.util.table.join( awful.button({}, 1, function() -- left click awful.spawn("your_action") update_widget() end) )) -- Wrap in container local final_widget = wibox.container.background( wibox.container.margin(my_widget_text, 2, 4), "#425047", -- use theme colors gears.shape.rounded_rect ) return final_widget ``` **Step 3: Register in theme.lua** ```lua -- Add at top of theme.lua with other requires local my_widget = require("themes.everforest.widgets_config.my_widget") -- OR for global widgets: local my_widget = require("widgets.my_widget.my_widget") -- Add to wibar in theme.connect() function, in the Right layout: s.mywibox:setup({ layout = wibox.layout.align.horizontal, { }, -- left { }, -- middle { -- right my_widget, wibox.widget.textbox(" "), -- ... other widgets ... }, }) ``` **Step 4: Use theme variables** - Colors: `theme.fg_widget`, `theme.bg_focus`, `theme.green`, `theme.red`, etc. - Font: `theme.font` - Icons path: `theme.dir .. "/icons/widgets/my_icon.png"` ### 7. Key Patterns & Best Practices 1. **Always return a single widget** - the top-level container 2. **Use `gears.shape.rounded_rect`** for consistency 3. **Wrap text widgets with markup** for color and font control 4. **Separate icon + text** with `wibox.layout.align.horizontal` 5. **Use margins** to add padding around widgets 6. **Poll with `gears.timer`** or use lain widgets for automatic updates 7. **Add mouse buttons** for interactivity (left=1, right=3, wheel=4/5) 8. **Prefer theme colors** over hardcoded colors for consistency 9. **Use `wibox.widget.textbox(" ")` as separators** between widgets 10. **Icons should be in theme directory** at `themes/{theme}/icons/widgets/` This is a well-structured system with clear separation between widget logic, theming, and registration!
